prompts.text: What responsibilities do individuals have when they witness misinformation spreading in their communities?
Should creative works generated by artificial intelligence be eligible for copyright protection?
Is the pursuit of work-life balance a realistic goal or a harmful myth?
Should public libraries evolve beyond books, or is their core mission worth preserving as-is?
Is failure a necessary ingredient for meaningful success?
Should consumers bear responsibility for the environmental impact of the products they buy?
Are large-scale international sporting events worth their economic and social costs?
Should schools prioritize teaching emotional intelligence over traditional academic subjects?
Is nostalgia a helpful force in society or a barrier to progress?
Should cities invest more in public spaces or affordable housing when budgets are limited?
How should governments balance public safety with individual privacy in the age of surveillance technology?
What policies would best encourage sustainable urban transportation?
How should schools adapt their curricula to prepare students for a world where AI handles routine tasks?
What is the most effective approach to reducing food waste at a national level?
How should countries handle the ethical sourcing of critical minerals needed for green technology?
What role should governments play in ensuring access to clean drinking water for all citizens?
How should societies address the growing digital divide between generations?
What policies would best support small businesses competing against large corporations?
How should public spaces be redesigned to foster stronger community connections?
What approaches would best reduce loneliness and social isolation in modern cities?
How should governments regulate the use of algorithms in hiring and lending decisions?
If you could add one subject to every school curriculum worldwide, what would it be and why?
If you could send a single message to every person on Earth simultaneously, what would you say?
If humans could no longer lie, how would society change?
If you were tasked with designing a new city from scratch, what would be your top three priorities?
If you could eliminate one global industry overnight, which would you choose and how would you handle the consequences?
If every person had to spend one year working in a different country, how would that change the world?
If you could make one scientific breakthrough happen tomorrow, what would it be?
If all decisions in your country were made by direct popular vote instead of elected representatives, what would improve and what would suffer?
If you could grant every child one skill at birth, what would it be and how would it reshape society?
If the average human lifespan doubled to 160 years, what social structures would need to change?
How is the rise of AI-generated content reshaping trust in media?
What are the long-term implications of the global shift toward renewable energy?
How is the growing popularity of remote learning changing the value of a traditional university degree?
What does the increasing use of facial recognition technology mean for civil liberties?
How are changing demographics reshaping the global economy?
What role should citizen journalism play alongside traditional media?
How is the growing mental health crisis among young people being addressed, and what more should be done?
What are the implications of private companies leading space exploration instead of governments?
How is the global water scarcity crisis likely to reshape international relations?
What effects is the rise of short-form video content having on how people learn and retain information?
How are supply chain disruptions changing the way countries think about self-sufficiency?
Is it ever morally justifiable to break a promise in order to prevent harm?
Does having more choices make people happier, or does it create paralysis and regret?
If a machine can perfectly replicate the experience of travel, is there still value in going to real places?
Is it possible to be truly selfless, or does every act of kindness serve the giver in some way?
Should we judge historical figures by the moral standards of their time or by today's standards?
What is the difference between knowledge and wisdom, and which matters more?
Does the concept of justice require punishment, or can it be achieved through restoration alone?
Is it more important to be authentic or to be kind when the two conflict?
Can a society be both perfectly equal and perfectly free at the same time?
Is there a moral obligation to be informed about world events, or is ignorance sometimes acceptable?
When two cultures have conflicting values, who decides which is right?
Is the desire to leave a legacy a sign of meaning or a sign of ego?
Describe a time you had to make a difficult decision with incomplete information. How did you approach it?
How would you handle a situation where your team disagrees with a decision made by leadership?
What is the most important quality in a leader, and how have you seen it demonstrated?
How do you approach giving constructive feedback to someone who is resistant to hearing it?
Describe how you would onboard a new team member to set them up for success in their first month.
How should professionals navigate the tension between personal values and organizational goals?
What strategies work best for maintaining productivity during periods of organizational change?
How do you decide when to advocate for your own idea versus supporting someone else's approach?
What does it mean to be a good mentor, and how does mentorship differ from management?
How would you rebuild trust within a team after a significant failure or setback?
What is the best way to communicate a complex or unpopular decision to a large group?
